title: Commercial Strip Mall FDC / Standpipe Supply

hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
FDC Supply Line A: 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to building FDC
FDC Supply Line B: 100 ft of 2.5-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to building FDC
Interior attack line: 150 ft of 2.5-inch hose from standpipe outlet to nozzle
appliances: Building FDC / standpipe system, 25 PSI system loss
sceneElements.elevation: Grade-level commercial occupancy, 0 PSI elevation correction

problems.title: Standpipe Attack Plus Sprinkler Support Flow
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying one interior standpipe attack line flowing 265 GPM and also assigning 250 GPM for sprinkler-system support through the FDC. What is the combined flow demand for the FDC operation?
problems.instructorExplanation: This problem separates flow demand from pump pressure. Add the standpipe attack flow and sprinkler-support flow; do not add duplicate flow just because multiple FDC hoses are connected.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count each FDC hose as a separate attack line. Add only the actual assigned flowing devices or system support demand.

problems.title: FDC Supply Line Friction Loss
problems.question: The building FDC is supplied by two equal 100 ft 2.5-inch lines. The single interior attack line requires 265 GPM total, so each FDC supply line carries 132.5 GPM. Using C = 2, what is the friction loss in each FDC supply line?
formulaBreakdown: The FDC is supplied by two equal supply lines.
Flow per FDC supply line = 265 ÷ 2 = 132.5 GPM.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
FDC supply line length = 100 ft = 1 hundred.
problems.instructorExplanation: Because the two FDC supply lines are equal, the total flow is split evenly between them. Each line carries 132.5 GPM, producing approximately 5 PSI friction loss.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate each FDC supply line using the full 265 GPM. The two equal lines split the flow before entering the FDC.

problems.title: Sixth-Floor Standpipe Elevation
problems.question: An interior standpipe attack is operating on the 6th floor. Use the FirePumpSim floor rule: 1st floor = 0 ft, each floor above the 1st adds 10 ft, and elevation adds 0.5 PSI per foot. What elevation pressure must be added?
formulaBreakdown: 6th floor is 5 floors above the 1st floor.
problems.instructorExplanation: Elevation is based on floors above the first floor, not the floor number itself.
problems.explainMistake: Do not multiply the 6th floor by 10 ft. The 1st floor is treated as 0 ft in this FirePumpSim rule.

problems.title: Total Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a grade-level standpipe attack through two equal 100 ft 2.5-inch FDC supply lines. Total attack flow is 265 GPM, so each FDC supply line carries 132.5 GPM. The interior attack line is 150 ft of 2.5-inch hose flowing 265 GPM through a 1⅛-inch smooth bore at 50 PSI. Add 25 PSI FDC/standpipe system loss and no elevation. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
FDC supply line friction loss = 5 PSI.
Interior attack line FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Interior attack line uses 2.5-inch hose, so C = 2.
Interior attack line length = 150 ft = 1.5 hundreds.
Round interior FL to nearest 5 PSI: 20 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: The pump must overcome the FDC supply hose friction loss, the interior attack line friction loss, nozzle pressure, and the FDC / standpipe system loss. The total required pump discharge pressure is 100 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate only the hose from the engine to the FDC. A complete standpipe supply calculation also includes the interior attack line, nozzle pressure, and standpipe system loss.

problems.title: Elevated Sprinkler FDC Support Pressure
problems.question: For a sprinkler-only FDC support operation, FirePumpSim uses a 150 PSI base setting. The sprinkler area being supported is on the 4th floor, so add 15 PSI elevation. No other losses are used. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: Base sprinkler FDC support setting = 150 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: Use the sprinkler-only support base setting, then add the stated elevation correction for the supported area.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate this like an interior handline. It is a sprinkler-support setting with the stated elevation correction.
